channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Peter Mandelson Released on Bail Following Misconduct Investigation
item.description: Lord Peter Mandelson, a former British politician, was released on bail by the Metropolitan Police following his arrest on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The 72-year-old returned to his Lon...

item.title: Life Under Fire in Belgorod: How the Ukraine War Has Reached Russia’s Border City
item.description: Four years after Vladimir Putin launched Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the war has reshaped daily life not only in Ukraine but also inside Russia itself, particularly in the border city of ...

item.title: Xiaomi Watch 5 Review: A Powerful Smartwatch with Impressive Battery Life
item.description: The Xiaomi Watch 5 is a high-end Wear OS smartwatch that combines power and performance. Featuring a 47mm stainless steel case, a 1.54-inch AMOLED display with flat sapphire glass, and a significant 9...

item.title: Troy Deeney's Premier League Team of the Week Selections
item.description: After each round of Premier League fixtures, football pundit Troy Deeney selects his team and manager of the week. This week, Dean Henderson of Crystal Palace earned recognition for a penalty save and...

item.title: Trump Implements 10% Global Tariff Following Supreme Court Ruling
item.description: US President Donald Trump has implemented a 10% global tariff after the Supreme Court blocked many of his previous sweeping import taxes. Following last week's ruling, Trump signed an executive order ...

item.title: Liverpool’s Forward Line Struggles: A Breakdown of Salah and Isak’s Decline
item.description: Liverpool's attacking struggles this season have been underlined by the disappointing performances of Mohamed Salah and Alexander Isak. Despite spending £200m on strikers last summer, the team has sco...

item.title: Zero-hours contracts reach record levels as government plans worker protections
item.description: The number of workers on zero-hours contracts in the UK has reached a record 1.23 million, driven largely by young people aged 16 to 24 and those not in full-time education. Analysis by the Work Found...

item.title: 40 Lesser-Known Natural Wonders You Won't Believe Exist
item.description: The natural world is full of surprising and lesser-known wonders that remain largely unexplored by most people. While famous landmarks like the Grand Canyon and the Great Barrier Reef dominate the hea...

item.title: 2026 Met Gala Theme Revealed: Fashion as Art
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala, scheduled for Monday, May 4, will celebrate the Costume Institute's spring exhibition, “Costume Art,” at the Metropolitan Museum of Art. This year’s dress code, “Fashion is Art,” in...

item.title: Green Party Leader Polanski Criticises UK for Not Condemning Trump's Attack on Iran
item.description: Green Party leader Zack Polanski has condemned the recent US military strikes on Iran, calling them 'illegal and unprovoked'. The strikes, which resulted in the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatolla...

item.title: Train CCTV Failures Hinder Investigation of Sexual Offences on UK Rail Network
item.description: A BBC investigation has revealed that sexual offenders on trains in England, Scotland, and Wales are often going untraced due to widespread issues with CCTV on the rail network. British Transport Poli...

item.title: Eddie Jones reflects on England's loss to Ireland and its impact on team morale
item.description: Former England head coach Eddie Jones has compared the atmosphere following England's recent Six Nations defeat against Ireland to the difficult end of his own tenure at Twickenham. England, aiming fo...

item.title: Manchester United Shocked by Early Crystal Palace Lead in Premier League Clash
item.description: Manchester United were stunned early in their Premier League match against Crystal Palace as the visitors took the lead within five minutes. Maxence Lacroix's brilliant header from a corner silenced t...

item.title: Mexico Deploys Thousands of Soldiers Following Death of Drug Lord El Mencho
item.description: Following the death of Nemesis Oseguera Cervantes, known as 'El Mencho', Mexican authorities have significantly increased military presence in parts of the country to curb violence. Captured by Mexica...

item.title: British Woman Faces Charges After Reporting Alleged Rape in Hong Kong
item.description: Isabel Rose, a 25-year-old British woman from Hackney, London, reported an alleged rape to Hong Kong police in early 2024 after meeting a man she had previously encountered while traveling in Asia. Th...

item.title: US-Israel Airstrike on Iran Hits School, Killing 51 Girls
item.description: In a disturbing escalation of military operations, an airstrike carried out by the United States and Israel has reportedly killed at least 51 girls at a primary school in the southern Iranian city of ...

item.title: Anthropic's AI Safety Challenges and the Consequences of Self-Regulation
item.description: Anthropic, an AI company founded in 2021 by Dario Amodei, is facing backlash after the Trump administration severed ties with it, citing national security concerns. The company’s refusal to use its te...

item.title: UK Regulator Fines Reddit £14m for Failing to Implement Effective Child Age Verification Measures
item.description: Reddit has been fined more than £14 million by the UK’s Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) following an investigation into the platform’s handling of children’s personal data. The data protection...

item.title: How Epstein Used Dirt on Powerful Figures to Secure His Protection
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein, the convicted sex offender with connections to powerful individuals across the political spectrum, exploited decades-old scandals for leverage. In a 2016 email to journalist Michael W...

item.title: Rangers and Celtic Battle in Old Firm Showdown with Tierney’s Header Narrowing the Gap
item.description: In a tense Old Firm derby, Celtic's hopes were revived by Kieran Tierney's header in the second half, pulling them back into the match after a dominant Rangers first half. With Hearts leading the Scot...
